parsed_review.summary: CP00-160 adds descriptor-only Master Data API/UI reference catalogs, fixtures, validators, tests, contract v0.5 bindings, H04/C04 packets, and CP00-161 handoff. No P0/P1 blockers were found. The reported P2 was confirmed as a false-positive by contract attestation and validator pass; two P3 robustness items are explicitly deferred.

boundary_assessment.notes: CP00-160 builds API and UI reference descriptors only; package code does not execute API handlers, network calls, UI rendering, Hermes, or Claude.

findings.title: Contract no_write_attestation may omit the three new CP160 safety keys required by the validator parity loop
findings.evidence: MASTER_DATA_CP160_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION (registry.js) introduces three net-new keys central to this pack's boundary claims: executes_api_handler:false, renders_ui:false, mutates_dom:false (in addition to builds_api_reference_catalog/builds_ui_surface_state_catalog/builds_api_fixture_descriptors). The contract validator loop was switched to iterate MASTER_DATA_CP160_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION and requires every key be present and equal in contract.no_write_attestation. The visible contract diff only adds builds_api_reference_catalog/builds_ui_surface_state_catalog/builds_api_fixture_descriptors and closes the no_write_attestation object (no trailing comma after builds_api_fixture_descriptors, immediately followed by hidden_source_fields), with no executes_api_handler/renders_ui/mutates_dom additions shown. These keys are CP160-specific (API/UI/DOM) and cannot plausibly pre-exist from CP156-CP159. If absent from the full file, npm run rp04:master-data:validate would fail with three 'no_write_attestation.<key> is required' errors. The reported gate pass implies they exist in the real file, so this is most likely a diff-display artifact, but it is the pack's core no-write safety claim and warrants explicit confirmation.
findings.recommended_action: Confirm contract.no_write_attestation contains executes_api_handler:false, renders_ui:false, mutates_dom:false (matching registry). If missing, add them to restore parity; otherwise no change needed.
findings.post_review_disposition: deferred/confirmed: full contract contains executes_api_handler:false, renders_ui:false, and mutates_dom:false; rp04:master-data:validate passed, so no code change required.

findings.title: Fixture omitted_fields is hardcoded rather than derived from actual sanitization
findings.evidence: In createMasterDataApiReferenceFixture, omitted_fields is a static literal (['items','internal_blocked_claim_refs'] for denied, ['secret','internal_blocked_claim_refs'] otherwise). sanitizeApiItem strips by MASTER_DATA_SERVICE_TAIL_BOUNDARY.hidden_output_fields plus internal_blocked_claim_refs, so the advertised omitted_fields descriptor can drift from what is actually stripped if hidden_output_fields changes. Descriptor-only impact only; no boundary or data-leak risk.
findings.recommended_action: Optionally derive omitted_fields from the keys actually removed by sanitizeApiItem so the descriptor stays in sync with the serialization guard.
findings.post_review_disposition: deferred: descriptor robustness hardening is tracked for a later pack and does not block this no-write reference closeout.

findings.title: Hidden-value self-check uses a single magic sentinel string
findings.evidence: createMasterDataApiReferenceCatalog asserts all_serialized_without_hidden_values via !JSON.stringify(fixture).includes('cp160_hidden_value_secret'). This only detects the one sentinel value injected by the fixture, not the full hidden_output_fields key set. Actual stripping (sanitizeApiItem) is robust; only the catalog's self-attestation is narrow.
findings.recommended_action: Optionally strengthen the self-check to assert absence of each hidden_output_fields key structurally rather than matching one literal value.
findings.post_review_disposition: deferred: descriptor robustness hardening is tracked for a later pack and does not block this no-write reference closeout.
